page: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated CPU server instance built on the x86_64 architecture, featuring 48 vCPUs derived from 24 physical cores of an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 188.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 3.91 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 960 GB of local SSD storage. It does not include a GPU or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the ccx63 demonstrates strong multi-core and single-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 73650.13 and a PassMark CPU score of 64305.59. Memory subsystem performance is characterized by a strong read-only small block cached bandwidth of 3511.81 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 95.71 GB/sec, though read-only large block uncached bandwidth drops to an average tier of 154.31 GB/sec. The instance excels in specialized tasks, achieving top-tier performance in text generation for small LLM models and strong results in software compilation, database operations, and Redis SET operations.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced resource density, the ccx63 offers high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring sustained, predictable compute power without the overhead of shared-core environments. The combination of 48 dedicated vCPUs, 188.0 GB of RAM, and local SSD storage makes this instance highly suitable for multi-threaded applications, software compilation pipelines, database hosting, static web serving, and moderate language model inference tasks.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated CPU server instance powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor with 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 188.0 GB of RAM, 960 GB of local SSD storage, and no GPU. The instance delivers strong multi-core performance, highlighted by a PassMark CPU score of 64305.59 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 73650.13. Memory performance is robust, with cached read bandwidth reaching 3511.81 GB/sec. The ccx63 is highly efficient for workloads requiring sustained, predictable performance, showing strong capabilities in software compilation, database operations, and Redis caching. It is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and moderate LLM inference.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated CPU server featuring 48 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 188.0 GB of RAM, and 960 GB of local SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core performance for databases and compilation.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC server with forty-eight vCPUs and local SSD storage, optimized for predictable multi-threaded workloads and database operations.

188.0 GB of system memory and 960 GB of local SSD storage
Strong multi-core performance with a PassMark CPU score of 64305.59
High-throughput memory bandwidth and rapid Redis SET operations
Optimized for software compilation, databases, and static web serving
